verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of the F1 catalytic core of mitochondrial ATP synthase. The F1 complex is a soluble protein assembly resident in the mitochondrial matrix, anchored to the membrane via the Fo proton-channel component but not itself membrane-spanning or surface-exposed. The protein is an inner-mitochondrial-membrane facing component with no documented translocation to the plasma membrane, no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no surface-exposure even under stress or cell-death states. The protein's biology is wholly mitochondrial bioenergetics.
